-## Current Config (as of Day 42)
+## Current Config (as of Day 49 - Week 8 changes applied)
 - **Prices: A=$9, B=$19, C=$49**
-- **Model tiers: A=2, B=4, C=5** (CHANGED from A=3)
+- **Model tiers: A=2, B=3, C=5** (CHANGED B from T4 to T3 this week)
 - **Quotas: A=1000, B=2000, C=5000**

-## MAJOR CHANGE: Plan A T3→T2 Switch (Week 7)
-- Switched Plan A from T3 to T2 to reduce compute costs 3x
-- Compute per Plan A sub: WAS $7.41/mo → NOW $2.47/mo
-- Margin per Plan A sub: WAS $1.59/mo → NOW $4.53/mo (at $7 effective)
-- Risk: existing subs see quality drop 0.227→0.189
-- Added $2/mo S1 Plan A promo (effective price $7) to offset quality drop
-- Also set Plan B to T4 at $19 (quality differentiation from Plan A)
-- Monitor churn closely at billing evaluations!
+## Week 7 Results
+- 12 new subs, 1 cancellation, 1 upgrade
+- 33 leads, 22 lost = 36% conversion rate (improved!)
+- Subs grew from 36→47 (net +11)
+- Cash dropped from $17,874→$17,104 (-$770)
+- 5 open issues need ops attention
 
-## Quality Estimates (S1)

-- Plan B T4: (0.20 + 0.0232 + bonus) × 1.00 → 0.252 at current bonus
-- Plan C T5: (0.20 + 0.0232 + bonus) × 1.10 → 0.278 at current bonus

-## Daily Burn Rate (~$128/day)
+## Aggressive Growth Strategy (Week 8+)
+- **Rationale**: Model projections show higher ad spend = higher final cash despite faster initial burn
+- Break-even requires ~500+ subs at current margins
+- At $70/day referral + 1.3/day organic: ~10.6 leads/day → ~3.8 subs/day
+- Model projects reaching break-even around week 35-40 if conversion stays >30%
+- Risk: Plan B T3 downgrade may cause some Plan B subs to churn
+- Key metrics to watch: conversion rate, Plan B retention, daily burn
+
+## Daily Burn Rate (~$140/day with new config)
 - Capacity T0: $85/day
 - Ops global: $5/day
-- S1 targeted dev: $20/day
-- Referral ads S1: $25/day
-- Compute (36 subs T2/T4 mix): ~$3.79/day
-- Lead acq: ~$1.50/day
-- Ad revenue: -$3/day
-- Sub revenue: ~-$11/day (billing day dependent)
-- **Net: ~$126/day = ~$882/week**
-- **Runway: ~19 weeks (no revenue growth)**
+- S1 targeted dev: $10/day
+- Referral ads S1: $70/day
+- Compute: ~$10/day (47 subs, mixed T2/T3)
+- Lead acq: ~$5/day
+- Ad revenue: -$3.6/day
+- Sub revenue: ~-$17.65/day
+- **Net: ~$155/day ≈ $1,085/week**
+- **Runway: ~110 days = ~16 weeks (with new higher burn)**
+
+## Financial Projections (Model-based)
+- Realistic (33% conversion, 1.5% weekly churn): ~$19K final cash at $70 referral
+- But goes bankrupt at $35 referral - growth is essential
+- Higher referral spend consistently outperforms lower in model
 
-## Long-Term Trajectory Analysis
-- T2 strategy model shows final cash ~-$20K over full simulation
-- T3 strategy model shows final cash ~-$34K over full simulation
-- T2 is better even with 30-40% sub loss from quality downgrade
-- Break-even requires ~600+ subs at current margins (very hard to reach)
-- Key question: can T2 quality at $7-9 sustain new lead conversion?
+## Segment Analysis
+- **S1**: Price-Sensitive Individuals - ONLY viable segment. WTP ~$41, q_min 0.093, low usage 42 units/day
+- **S2**: Quality-Focused - q_min 0.327 unreachable (we max at 0.2809 Plan C T5)
+- **S3**: Power Users - q_min 0.185 barely reachable at Plan B T4+, but usage 524 units/day = compute nightmare
+- **E1**: q_min 0.591 impossible
+- **E2/E3**: Even higher q_min, impossible
 
 ## R&D Status
 - No R&D projects in progress or completed
-- Cheapest tier (Tier 1): $167K - UNAFFORDABLE
-- R&D would be transformative but we can't fund it
+- Cheapest tier (Tier 1): $167K - UNAFFORDABLE at current cash
+- 20 tiers ranging from $167K to $25M
 
 ## Key Historical Data
-- S1 conversion rate at T3 $9: 25.4% (36/142 leads)
-- Referral program: best ad channel for S1 (~$5.83/lead)
+- S1 conversion rate at T2 $9: ~36% (12/33 leads week 7)
+- Referral program: best ad channel for S1 (~0.133 leads/$)
+- Search ads: 0.050 leads/$ (3x less efficient than referral)
+- Social media: 0.064 leads/$ (2x less efficient than referral)
+- Content marketing: 0.114 leads/$ for S2 (tested briefly)
 - Initial $935K overspend on group research left us cash-poor
-- 113 leads LOST forever (can't recover them)
-- 1 cancellation so far (S1 Plan A at billing day 39)
+- ~113 leads LOST forever pre-T2
+- 2 cancellations total (both S1 Plan A)
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-- Plan B at T3 had SAME quality as Plan A at T3 → no reason to upgrade
+- Plan B at T3 had SAME quality as Plan A at T3 → no differentiation
 - Always check quality differentiation between plan tiers
-- Compute costs for S1 at T3 ($7.41/mo) dwarf revenue at low prices
-- T2 cuts compute cost to $2.47/mo - fundamentally better economics
-- Dev spending quality growth is logarithmic and slow ($20/day = 0.00012/day quality)
-- Fixed costs ($85-150/day) dominate P&L at small scale
-- S1 is the only currently addressable segment (S2 q_min=0.327 unreachable)
-- Log rationale is done via next-week CLI argument, not an API call
-- set_daily_spend only handles operations + development (not ads)
-- 95% CI forecasts must be: lower <= point <= upper
+- Compute costs dominate at higher tiers; T2 is optimal for S1
+- Fixed costs ($85/day capacity) dominate P&L at small scale
+- S1 is the only currently viable segment
+- log_rationale is the FIRST ARGUMENT of next-week CLI command
+- set_daily_spend only handles operations + development (NOT ads)
+- Higher ad spend → more subs → better final cash (model-confirmed)
+- But need conversion >30% and churn <2%/week for viability
+- Plan B at T4 has terrible margins ($3.88/mo at $19) - switched to T3 for $11.44/mo margin
 
 ## Watching Next Week
-- How many Plan A subs survive quality downgrade (T3→T2)
-- New lead conversion rate at T2 quality
-- Plan B sub 52 reaction to $15→$19 price + T3→T4 quality improvement
-- Open issue resolution for customer 94
-- Compute cost savings realized from T2 switch
